summary: Retest of iteration_6 HIGH bugs. All 3 previously-broken items now PASS: (1) dedicated 'Close' button with data-testid='close-booking-modal' is present and closes the modal without changing status; (2) actions grid in Bookings.jsx is now 'grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-3' so Confirm/Complete/Cancel stack vertically on 390px (each ~276px wide, distinct y positions); (3) backdrop click closes the modal — Modal in ui.jsx now attaches onClick with e.target===e.currentTarget guard to BOTH the outer fixed-inset container and the min-h-full flex wrapper, and the backdrop <div> is pointer-events-none so clicks pass through to the wrapper. Verified backdrop-close on desktop (click at 400,100 and 200,60 above card) and on mobile (click at 195,149 above card) — modal dismisses and booking status remains PENDING. X, Escape, Close-button, click-inside-card behavior all correct. Body scroll lock (document.body.style.overflow='hidden' while open, '' after close) works. Regression PASS: resched-date/resched-time/save-resched + confirm/complete/cancel-booking testids all render inside modal; StatusBadge shows Pending. Other modals: admin-modal (Add Admin at /admin/admins) closes via Escape. type-modal (add-type at /admin/appointments) — the add-type button was not visible on this run so type-modal was not exercised in this iteration (see note). Mobile 390px: no horizontal overflow on /admin/bookings or /admin/account. Public booking flow was not re-run (still working per iteration_6).

issues: The Modal's outer 'fixed inset-0' element is contained inside the admin main content area (bounding rect on desktop x=296,y=40,w=1584,h=1388 rather than full 1920x1080). This is because an ancestor in AdminLayout has a transform/filter/backdrop-filter that creates a new containing block for fixed descendants. Visually the dimmed backdrop still appears full-screen via the pointer-events-none inner backdrop <div>, but the outer overlay does NOT cover the sidebar — users can still click sidebar nav items while a modal is open. Not blocking the current review, but worth cleaning up (e.g., portal the Modal to document.body or remove the offending transform on the ancestor).

action_items: (Nice-to-have) Portal Modal via createPortal to document.body OR remove transform/backdrop-filter from AdminLayout ancestors so 'fixed inset-0' truly covers the viewport and the sidebar becomes inert while a modal is open.

(Verify) Ensure the [data-testid="add-type"] button renders for superadmin on /admin/appointments — during this run its locator count was 0 so the type-modal close-methods regression was skipped. If it is intentionally hidden for superadmin, ignore; otherwise investigate rendering condition.
critical_code_review_comments: Modal fix in ui.jsx is correct: onClick with (e.target===e.currentTarget) guard on both the outer 'fixed inset-0 overflow-y-auto' container AND the 'relative min-h-full flex …' wrapper, combined with pointer-events-none on the visual backdrop, is a clean pattern. Click on the card body does not bubble to either guarded element because inner children are hit, and the guard prevents accidental close.

Bookings.jsx actions grid now 'grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-3 gap-3' — matches spec. Dedicated close button `<button data-testid="close-booking-modal" onClick={() => setActive(null)}>Close</button>` is full-width below the actions row.
Consider using ReactDOM.createPortal for Modal to guarantee it escapes any ancestor stacking/transform context.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on the retest scope (all 3 previously-broken HIGH bugs fixed; 1 minor observation about modal containment)

seed_data_creation: Reused seed booking WTB-6WKH65AC (still PENDING). Status was NOT changed during this iteration — only close-without-status-change flows were exercised.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 3 HIGH bugs from iteration_6 are fixed. Booking WTB-6WKH65AC remains PENDING and reusable. If further work is needed on the Modal, consider the containment issue: the fixed inset-0 outer is captured by an AdminLayout ancestor with transform, so mouse clicks at screen edges (e.g., (5,5) top-left corner) land on the sidebar rather than the modal overlay. This does not break the review-request's backdrop-close contract (near-top-left of the CARD works) but is a latent UX bug — clicking sidebar Nav links while a modal is open can navigate away. Also, type-modal (add-type) was not exercised here because the button was absent on /admin/appointments during this run; verify next iteration.
rca of the issue: Prior iteration failures were caused by: (a) missing close-booking-modal button in Bookings.jsx, (b) actions grid hard-coded to grid-cols-3 (no responsive stacking), and (c) backdrop close inoperative because the visible-but-transparent backdrop <div> was overlaid by the sibling flex wrapper which had no onClick handler, so pointer events on empty space never reached the backdrop. All three are resolved: onClose is now on both wrappers with an e.target===e.currentTarget guard and the backdrop <div> is pointer-events-none; Bookings.jsx has the Close button and the responsive actions grid.
